questions.question_text: Which notation means line $AB$ is parallel to line $CD$?

options.why: Uses the perpendicular symbol instead of the parallel symbol.
States equal lengths rather than a parallel line relationship.

questions.question_text: Which notation means line $EF$ is perpendicular to line $GH$?

options.why: Uses the parallel symbol instead of the perpendicular symbol.
States equal lengths rather than a right-angle relationship.

questions.question_text: What does a small square at the intersection of two lines show?

options.why: Confuses a right-angle square with matching parallel arrows.
Confuses a right-angle square with equal-interval tick marks.
Treats an intersection mark as the definition of a transversal.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,1)--(2,1) node[right] {$p$};\draw (-2,-1)--(2,-1) node[right] {$q$};\draw (-1.2,-1.7)--(0.7,1.7) node[above] {$t$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Which line is the transversal?

options.why: Chooses one crossed line rather than the line crossing both.
Chooses one crossed line rather than the line crossing both.
Treats the two crossed lines as the transversal themselves.
